tripOverview.intro: London captivates with its perfect blend of historic charm and modern romance, from royal parks to cozy pubs along the Thames. This vibrant metropolis offers endless opportunities for couples to explore world-class museums, charming markets, and intimate dining experiences.
tripOverview.vibe: A whirlwind romantic adventure through London's most iconic sights, charming markets, and cozy vegetarian eateries perfect for couples.

destinationOverview.bestTime: April offers mild spring weather with blooming flowers and fewer crowds, perfect for exploring parks and outdoor markets.

attractions.name: Tower Bridge
attractions.desc: Iconic Victorian bascule bridge with stunning Thames views.

attractions.name: British Museum
attractions.desc: World-renowned museum housing treasures from global civilizations.

attractions.name: Hyde Park
attractions.desc: Royal park perfect for romantic strolls and Speaker's Corner.

attractions.name: Covent Garden
attractions.desc: Historic market area with street performers and boutique shopping.

destinationOverview.transportation: London's extensive Underground system connects all major attractions with contactless payment accepted. Black cabs are iconic but pricey, while Uber operates throughout the city. Walking between nearby attractions is often faster and more romantic than using transport.
destinationOverview.tip: Download Citymapper for real-time transport updates and always stand right on escalators - Londoners take this seriously!
destinationOverview.safety: London is very safe for LGBTQ+ travelers with Soho being the main gay district and most areas welcoming to all couples.
destinationOverview.airaloText: Stay connected with affordable eSIM data plans, perfect for navigating London's streets and sharing romantic moments instantly.

days.intro: Start your London adventure exploring the historic City and indulging in Borough Market's incredible food scene.

morning.name: Arrive in London
morning.desc: Land at Heathrow and take the Elizabeth Line to central London.

morning.name: Check into Hotel
morning.desc: Drop bags and freshen up at your accommodation.

morning.name: Tower Bridge
morning.desc: Explore the iconic bridge and glass floor walkway with Thames views.

afternoon.name: Borough Market
afternoon.desc: Sample artisanal foods and vegetarian delights at London's oldest market.

afternoon.name: Southwark Cathedral
afternoon.desc: Visit London's oldest Gothic church with beautiful architecture.

afternoon.name: Thames Path Walk
afternoon.desc: Romantic riverside stroll from Borough to London Bridge.

evening.name: Dinner at Champor-Champor
evening.desc: Authentic vegetarian Thai cuisine in a cozy romantic setting.

evening.name: Pub at The George Inn
evening.desc: Historic galleried coaching inn for traditional British ales.

days.tip: Borough Market is busiest on weekends - arrive early for the best selection and shorter queues at popular stalls.

days.intro: Immerse yourselves in British culture with world-class museums and romantic park strolls.

morning.name: Breakfast at Dishoom
morning.desc: Bombay-style cafe serving excellent vegetarian Indian breakfast.

morning.name: British Museum
morning.desc: Explore ancient treasures including the Rosetta Stone and Egyptian mummies.

morning.name: Russell Square
morning.desc: Peaceful garden square perfect for a romantic coffee break.

afternoon.name: Lunch at Rasa
afternoon.desc: South Indian vegetarian restaurant with authentic Kerala cuisine.

afternoon.name: Hyde Park
afternoon.desc: Stroll through Speaker's Corner and along the Serpentine Lake.

afternoon.name: Diana Memorial
afternoon.desc: Visit the touching memorial in Hyde Park's beautiful gardens.

evening.name: Dinner at Mildreds
evening.desc: Popular vegetarian restaurant known for creative plant-based dishes.

evening.name: Comedy Store
evening.desc: Legendary comedy club in Leicester Square for evening entertainment.

days.tip: Book British Museum timed entry slots online in advance - it's free but gets very busy, especially around the Rosetta Stone.

days.intro: Experience London from the water and explore vibrant cultural neighborhoods perfect for couples.

morning.name: Coffee at Workshop Coffee
morning.desc: Artisanal coffee roastery in trendy Fitzrovia neighborhood.

morning.name: Thames River Cruise
morning.desc: Scenic boat journey from Westminster to Greenwich with commentary.

morning.name: Greenwich Market
morning.desc: Browse unique crafts and sample international vegetarian street food.

afternoon.name: Cutty Sark
afternoon.desc: Explore the world's last surviving tea clipper ship.

afternoon.name: Greenwich Park
afternoon.desc: Climb to Royal Observatory for panoramic London views.

afternoon.name: Return to Central London
afternoon.desc: Take DLR back to Bank and explore the City area.

evening.name: Dinner at Vanilla Black
evening.desc: Upscale vegetarian fine dining with innovative tasting menus.

evening.name: Rooftop at Radio
evening.desc: LGBTQ+ friendly rooftop bar with stunning city skyline views.

days.tip: Greenwich's Prime Meridian Line is free to straddle outside the gates - save money and still get that iconic photo!

days.intro: Spend your final morning shopping in Covent Garden before heading to the airport.

morning.name: Breakfast at Jack's Cafe
morning.desc: Local favorite serving hearty vegetarian breakfast options.

morning.name: Covent Garden Market
morning.desc: Watch street performers and browse unique boutique shops.

morning.name: Neal's Yard
morning.desc: Colorful courtyard perfect for Instagram photos and cheese shopping.

afternoon.name: Lunch at Food for Thought
afternoon.desc: Tiny basement vegetarian restaurant serving wholesome homemade food.

afternoon.name: Last-minute Shopping
afternoon.desc: Pick up souvenirs at Covent Garden's unique independent stores.

afternoon.name: Depart to Airport
afternoon.desc: Take Elizabeth Line directly to Heathrow for evening flight.

evening.name: Airport Departure
evening.desc: Board evening flight back to Philadelphia with wonderful memories.

days.tip: Covent Garden street performers work for tips - a pound or two shows appreciation for their entertaining acts!

food.intro: London's vegetarian scene has exploded with innovative plant-based restaurants, traditional Indian cuisine, and charming cafes perfect for romantic dining.
food.tip: Many pubs serve excellent vegetarian meals alongside drinks - look for gastropubs for the best food quality and romantic atmosphere.

neighborhoods.name: Covent Garden
neighborhoods.desc: Heart of London's theater district with street performers and boutique shopping
neighborhoods.whyStay: Walking distance to major attractions and romantic restaurants

neighborhoods.name: South Bank
neighborhoods.desc: Modern riverside area with stunning Thames views and cultural attractions
neighborhoods.whyStay: Near Tate Modern, Borough Market, and romantic riverside walks

neighborhoods.name: Fitzrovia
neighborhoods.desc: Trendy neighborhood with excellent restaurants and proximity to museums
neighborhoods.whyStay: Great dining scene and easy access to British Museum and Oxford Street

budget.name: Premier Inn London County Hall
budget.vibe: Modern chain hotel with Thames views

midRange.name: The Z Hotel Piccadilly
midRange.vibe: Boutique hotel in prime West End location

midRange.name: Hub by Premier Inn Covent Garden
midRange.vibe: Modern compact rooms in theater district

luxury.name: The Savoy
luxury.vibe: Legendary luxury hotel with Art Deco glamour

luxury.name: Covent Garden Hotel
luxury.vibe: Boutique luxury in the heart of theater land

hotels.tip: Book hotels near tube stations for easy transport access - London traffic makes walking or using the Underground much faster than taxis during rush hours.

mustDo.name: Borough Market Food Tour
mustDo.desc: Sample artisanal British and international foods

mustDo.name: Thames Evening Cruise
mustDo.desc: Romantic sunset views of London landmarks

mustDo.name: British Museum Highlights
mustDo.desc: World treasures in magnificent historic setting

mustDo.name: Hyde Park Picnic
mustDo.desc: Romantic outdoor dining in royal parkland

mustDo.name: Covent Garden Street Performance
mustDo.desc: Free entertainment in historic market square

getYourGuide.name: London Food Walking Tour
getYourGuide.desc: Discover the best vegetarian and traditional British foods with a local guide through Borough Market and beyond.

getYourGuide.name: Thames River Evening Cruise
getYourGuide.desc: Romantic sunset cruise with commentary passing Tower Bridge, London Eye, and Houses of Parliament.

getYourGuide.name: Royal London Walking Tour
getYourGuide.desc: Explore royal palaces, parks, and landmarks with stories of British monarchy and LGBTQ+ royal history.

hiddenGems.name: Neal's Yard
hiddenGems.desc: Colorful courtyard hidden in Covent Garden with organic shops and wholefood cafes perfect for couples.

hiddenGems.name: Leadenhall Market
hiddenGems.desc: Victorian covered market featured in Harry Potter films, ideal for romantic strolls and photography.

hiddenGems.name: Kyoto Garden Holland Park
hiddenGems.desc: Peaceful Japanese garden with waterfalls and koi ponds, a serene escape from London's bustle.

packing.title: April in London
packing.intro: Pack layers for unpredictable spring weather with occasional showers and temperatures ranging from 45-60°F.
packing.luggage: Pack light in a carry-on if possible - London has excellent shopping if you need to buy anything you forgot.
packing.tip: Bring a compact umbrella - London weather is famously unpredictable and you'll want to keep exploring even in light rain.

bookTrip.intro: Flights from Philadelphia to London typically cost $400-600 per person in April, making this an excellent value destination for couples.
